_meta.notes: Field names and values match Surge Discovery create form (Full Mode, 9 steps)

tagline: TON-native encrypted memory persistence for AI agents inside Telegram mini-apps.
full_description: Syncpad solves the statelessness problem of AI-powered TON mini-apps by providing a wallet-keyed memory layer that stores structured agent context as versioned, encrypted blobs anchored to TON's cell-based data model. Each memory shard is encrypted client-side using AES-256-GCM keyed to the user's TON wallet before being committed to TON Storage, with a content hash written to a FunC smart contract that emits auditable on-chain events for every write. Developers integrate via a TypeScript SDK

logo.note: Surge requires PNG or JPG. Convert SVG → PNG before upload.

dev_tools: TON Storage for encrypted blob persistence

pitch_deck.note: Surge requires PDF. Convert PPTX → PDF before upload.

pitch_video.note: Must be public/unlisted YouTube URL. Max 800 seconds.

team_description: The founding team has combined experience in TON smart contract development and Telegram bot infrastructure, with prior work shipping production FunC contracts and TypeScript SDKs used by mini-app developers across the TON ecosystem.

roadmap.goal: Ship a working prototype of the core memory write/read cycle on TON testnet with client-side encryption.
deliverables: FunC smart contract deployed to TON testnet that anchors memory content hashes and emits write events
Client-side AES-256-GCM encryption module keyed to TON wallet address with TL-B cell serialization
Proof-of-concept TypeScript SDK with write and read functions tested against a single demo Telegram mini-app

roadmap.goal: Launch public beta SDK on TON mainnet with audit log UI and onboard 10 external mini-app developer teams.
deliverables: Production FunC contract deployed to TON mainnet with memory versioning, TTL enforcement, and access revocation logic
Full TypeScript SDK release (write, read, diff) published to npm with TON Connect 2.0 wallet auth integration
Web-based memory audit dashboard allowing users to inspect per-app write events linked to their TON wallet

roadmap.goal: Enable cross-app memory portability with wallet-signed consent and reach 50,000 active memory wallets.
deliverables: Cross-app memory sharing protocol: wallet-signed consent transactions granting scoped read access to external mini-apps
Memory diff and conflict resolution logic in the SDK for multi-app concurrent writes to shared context shards
Developer portal with usage analytics, per-app memory commit counts, and mainnet cost estimator for teams
